originalTitle: Senegal government alleges corruption over Afcon
title: Senegal government alleges corruption over Afcon
content: The Senegalese government calls for an "independent international investigation" at the Confederation of African Football after it stripped Senegal of the Africa Cup of Nations title.

originalTitle: King greets Nigerian president for first UK state visit in 37 years
title: Big Smiles for Nigeria's President in the UK!
content:
Hello, amazing friends all across Africa! It's your super-duper news reporter here, ready to share some really exciting news! Today, we're talking about a very special visitor from our own continent, Africa, making big waves far away!

A Royal Welcome!

Guess what? The King of a country called the United Kingdom, his name is King Charles, just had a very important meeting! He met our very own President from Nigeria! Wow, isn't that cool? It was a super warm welcome for President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. This meeting happened in a lovely place called Windsor. It's in the United Kingdom, which is often called the UK for short. The King and the President shook hands and smiled. They were very happy to see each other!

Who is King Charles?

So, who is King Charles? He is like the main leader of the United Kingdom. He lives in a very famous palace! Kings and Queens have been leaders there for a very, very long time. His job is to help his country and meet important people from all over the world. Did you know? The United Kingdom is a country made up of England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland! It's famous for its red double-decker buses and big clocks like Big Ben! He wears a shiny crown for special events. He is like the royal father figure for his country. It's a very important job!

Meet President Tinubu of Nigeria!

Now, let's talk about our wonderful visitor, President Bola Ahmed Tinubu! He is the President of Nigeria, which is a HUGE country right here in Africa! Nigeria is famous for having lots and lots of people, beautiful cultures, and amazing music. A President is like the big boss of a country. He helps make sure everyone has schools, hospitals, and good roads. President Tinubu works very hard to make Nigeria a better place for all children and grown-ups. Did you know? Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! People there speak many different languages, like Yoruba, Igbo, and Hausa, but English is also very common. It's a country full of energy and bright colours!

What is a State Visit?

This meeting wasn't just any regular meeting. It was called a 'state visit'! What does that mean? It means it's a super-duper important visit where leaders of two countries meet to talk about big things. It's like when you have a very special playdate with your best friend, but for countries! They talk about how their countries can be friends, help each other, and work together. Maybe they talk about trade, which means selling and buying things, or how to keep the world safe. When leaders go on a state visit, it shows that their countries want to be good friends. They want to learn from each other. It's a way to build strong bridges of friendship across the world.

A Very Long Time Ago!

Here's something amazing about this visit! It's the FIRST time a President from Nigeria has made a state visit to the UK in 37 years! Can you imagine 37 years? That's like grown-up age for many people! If you are in Class 2, you are maybe 7 or 8 years old. So, 37 years is like waiting for your birthday almost five times over and over again! Wow! It shows how very special this visit is. It's a fresh start for strong friendship between Nigeria and the United Kingdom. It makes history!

More Royal Friends!

But wait, there's more royal news! King Charles wasn't the only one who met President Tinubu. His son, Prince William, and Prince William's wife, Princess Catherine, also met him! They are called the Prince and Princess of Wales. They are also very important people in the Royal Family. They went to a beautiful hotel in Windsor to say hello to President Tinubu. This shows that the whole royal family is happy to welcome our President. They want to show that they care about friendship with Nigeria. It's a team effort to build good relationships!

Why This Friendship Matters

Why is it so important for countries like Nigeria and the UK to be friends? Well, when countries are friends, they can help each other! They can share ideas on how to build better schools for kids. They can talk about how to make sure everyone has good doctors and hospitals. They can also work together to protect our planet. Friendship between countries makes the world a better, safer place for everyone, especially for children like you! Our President and the King talked about making life better for people. They talked about peace and progress. It’s like two big families deciding to work together for a better future!

Our Hope for the Future!

So, this visit of President Tinubu to meet King Charles is a very big and happy event! It shows that Nigeria and the United Kingdom want to be strong friends for many, many years to come. It’s a good sign for Africa and for the whole world! Let's all cheer for friendship and working together! Maybe one day, you too will grow up to be a leader, making friends with people from all over the world! Keep shining bright, African stars!
summary: King Charles of the United Kingdom gave a warm welcome to President Tinubu of Nigeria during a very important 'state visit,' the first of its kind in 37 years, to build friendship between their countries.
whyMatters: This meeting is super important because it helps Nigeria and the United Kingdom be good friends, share ideas, and work together to make life better for everyone, especially kids, for a brighter future.
